name: adafruit-pureio

metadata.name: Adafruit-PureIO
metadata.summary: Pure python (i.e. no native extensions) access to Linux IO    including I2C and SPI. Drop in replacement for smbus and spidev modules.

documentation:
# Introduction

```{image} https://readthedocs.org/projects/adafruit-pureio/badge/?version=latest
:alt: Documentation Status
:target: https://adafruit-pureio.readthedocs.io/en/latest/
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/discord/327254708534116352.svg
:alt: Discord
:target: https://adafru.it/discord
```

```{image} https://github.com/adafruit/Adafruit_Python_PureIO/workflows/Build%20CI/badge.svg
:alt: Build Status
:target: https://github.com/adafruit/Adafruit_Python_PureIO/actions
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/badge/code%20style-black-000000.svg
:alt: 'Code Style: Black'
:target: https://github.com/psf/black
```

Pure python (i.e. no native extensions) access to Linux IO including I2C and SPI. Drop in replacement for smbus and spidev modules.

# Dependencies

This driver depends on:

- Python 3.5 or higher

# Installing from PyPI

On supported GNU/Linux systems like the Raspberry Pi, you can install the driver locally [from
PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/Adafruit-PureIO/). To install for current user:

```shell
pip3 install Adafruit-PureIO
```

To install system-wide (this may be required in some cases):

```shell
sudo pip3 install Adafruit-PureIO
```

To install in a virtual environment in your current project:

```shell
mkdir project-name && cd project-name
python3 -m venv .env
source .env/bin/activate
pip3 install Adafruit-PureIO
```

# Contributing

Contributions are welcome! Please read our [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/adafruit/Adafruit_Python_PureIO/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)
before contributing to help this project stay welcoming.

# Documentation

For information on building library documentation, please check out [this guide](https://learn.adafruit.com/creating-and-sharing-a-circuitpython-library/sharing-our-docs-on-readthedocs#sphinx-5-1).

modules.name: Adafruit_PureIO.spi
documentation:
`Adafruit_PureIO.spi`
================================================================================

Pure python (i.e. no native extensions) access to Linux IO SPI interface that is
similar to the SpiDev API. Based heavily on https://github.com/tomstokes/python-spi/.

* Author(s): Tom Stokes, Melissa LeBlanc-Williams

Implementation Notes
--------------------

**Software and Dependencies:**

* Linux and Python 3.5 or Higher
SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2020 Melissa LeBlanc-Williams for Adafruit Industries
SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT

functions.name: Adafruit_PureIO.spi._ioc_encode
documentation:
ioctl command encoding helper function
Calculates the appropriate spidev ioctl op argument given the direction,
command number, and argument structure in python's struct.pack format.
Returns a tuple of the calculated op and the struct.pack format
See Linux kernel source file /include/uapi/asm/ioctl.h

classes.name: Adafruit_PureIO.spi.SPI

methods.name: Adafruit_PureIO.spi.SPI.__init__
documentation: Create spidev interface object.
pylint: disable=too-many-arguments

methods.name: Adafruit_PureIO.spi.SPI._ioctl
documentation:
ioctl helper function.

Performs an ioctl on self.handle. If the ioctl is an SPI read type
ioctl, returns the result value.
pylint: enable=too-many-arguments

documentation: Helper function to get specific spidev mode bits

methods.name: Adafruit_PureIO.spi.SPI._set_mode_field
documentation: Helper function to set a spidev mode bit

methods.name: Adafruit_PureIO.spi.SPI.phase
documentation: SPI clock phase bit

methods.name: Adafruit_PureIO.spi.SPI.polarity
documentation: SPI polarity bit

methods.name: Adafruit_PureIO.spi.SPI.cs_high
documentation: SPI chip select active level

methods.name: Adafruit_PureIO.spi.SPI.lsb_first
documentation: Bit order of SPI word transfers

methods.name: Adafruit_PureIO.spi.SPI.three_wire
documentation: SPI 3-wire mode

methods.name: Adafruit_PureIO.spi.SPI.loop
documentation: SPI loopback mode

methods.name: Adafruit_PureIO.spi.SPI.no_cs
documentation: No chipselect. Single device on bus.

methods.name: Adafruit_PureIO.spi.SPI.ready
documentation: Slave pulls low to pause

methods.name: Adafruit_PureIO.spi.SPI.max_speed_hz
documentation:
Maximum SPI transfer speed in Hz.

Note that the controller cannot necessarily assign the requested
speed.

methods.name: Adafruit_PureIO.spi.SPI.bits_per_word
documentation:
Number of bits per word of SPI transfer.

A value of 0 is equivalent to 8 bits per word

methods.name: Adafruit_PureIO.spi.SPI.mode
documentation: Mode that SPI is currently running in

methods.name: Adafruit_PureIO.spi.SPI.writebytes
documentation: Perform half-duplex SPI write.

methods.name: Adafruit_PureIO.spi.SPI.readbytes
documentation: Perform half-duplex SPI read as a binary string

methods.name: Adafruit_PureIO.spi.SPI.transfer
documentation: Perform full-duplex SPI transfer

This class is similar to SpiDev, but instead of opening and closing
for each call, it is set up on initialization making it fast.
pylint: disable=too-many-instance-attributes, too-many-branches

modules.name: Adafruit_PureIO.smbus
documentation:
`Adafruit_PureIO.smbus`
================================================================================

Pure python (i.e. no native extensions) access to Linux IO I2C interface that mimics the
Python SMBus API.

* Author(s): Tony DiCola, Lady Ada, Melissa LeBlanc-Williams

Implementation Notes
--------------------

**Software and Dependencies:**

* Linux and Python 3.5 or Higher
SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2016 Tony DiCola for Adafruit Industries
SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT

functions.name: Adafruit_PureIO.smbus.make_i2c_rdwr_data
documentation:
Utility function to create and return an i2c_rdwr_ioctl_data structure
populated with a list of specified I2C messages.  The messages parameter
should be a list of tuples which represent the individual I2C messages to
send in this transaction.  Tuples should contain 4 elements: address value,
flags value, buffer length, ctypes c_uint8 pointer to buffer.
pylint: enable=invalid-name,too-few-public-methods
pylint: disable=attribute-defined-outside-init

variables.name: Adafruit_PureIO.smbus.I2C_M_RECV_LEN

classes.name: Adafruit_PureIO.smbus.i2c_msg
documentation: Linux i2c_msg struct.
ctypes versions of I2C structs defined by kernel.
Tone down pylint for the Python classes that mirror C structs.
pylint: disable=invalid-name,too-few-public-methods

classes.name: Adafruit_PureIO.smbus.i2c_rdwr_ioctl_data
documentation: Linux i2c data struct.

classes.name: Adafruit_PureIO.smbus.SMBus

methods.name: Adafruit_PureIO.smbus.SMBus.__init__
documentation:
Create a new smbus instance.  Bus is an optional parameter that
specifies the I2C bus number to use, for example 1 would use device
/dev/i2c-1.  If bus is not specified then the open function should be
called to open the bus.

methods.name: Adafruit_PureIO.smbus.SMBus.__del__
documentation: Clean up any resources used by the SMBus instance.

methods.name: Adafruit_PureIO.smbus.SMBus.__enter__
documentation: Context manager enter function.

methods.name: Adafruit_PureIO.smbus.SMBus.__exit__
documentation: Context manager exit function, ensures resources are cleaned up.

methods.name: Adafruit_PureIO.smbus.SMBus.open
documentation: Open the smbus interface on the specified bus.

methods.name: Adafruit_PureIO.smbus.SMBus.close
documentation:
Close the smbus connection.  You cannot make any other function
calls on the bus unless open is called!

methods.name: Adafruit_PureIO.smbus.SMBus._select_device
documentation: Set the address of the device to communicate with on the I2C bus.

methods.name: Adafruit_PureIO.smbus.SMBus.read_byte
documentation: Read a single byte from the specified device.

methods.name: Adafruit_PureIO.smbus.SMBus.read_bytes
documentation: Read many bytes from the specified device.

methods.name: Adafruit_PureIO.smbus.SMBus.read_byte_data
documentation: Read a single byte from the specified cmd register of the device.

methods.name: Adafruit_PureIO.smbus.SMBus.read_word_data
documentation:
Read a word (2 bytes) from the specified cmd register of the device.
Note that this will interpret data using the endianness of the processor
running Python (typically little endian)!

methods.name: Adafruit_PureIO.smbus.SMBus.read_block_data
documentation:
Perform a block read from the specified cmd register of the device.
The amount of data read is determined by the first byte send back by
the device.  Data is returned as a bytearray.

methods.name: Adafruit_PureIO.smbus.SMBus.read_i2c_block_data
documentation:
Perform a read from the specified cmd register of device.  Length number
of bytes (default of 32) will be read and returned as a bytearray.

methods.name: Adafruit_PureIO.smbus.SMBus.write_quick
documentation: Write a single byte to the specified device.

methods.name: Adafruit_PureIO.smbus.SMBus.write_byte
documentation: Write a single byte to the specified device.

methods.name: Adafruit_PureIO.smbus.SMBus.write_bytes
documentation: Write many bytes to the specified device. buf is a bytearray

methods.name: Adafruit_PureIO.smbus.SMBus.write_byte_data
documentation: Write a byte of data to the specified cmd register of the device.

methods.name: Adafruit_PureIO.smbus.SMBus.write_word_data
documentation:
Write a word (2 bytes) of data to the specified cmd register of the
device.  Note that this will write the data in the endianness of the
processor running Python (typically little endian)!

methods.name: Adafruit_PureIO.smbus.SMBus.write_block_data
documentation:
Write a block of data to the specified cmd register of the device.
The amount of data to write should be the first byte inside the vals
string/bytearray and that count of bytes of data to write should follow
it.

methods.name: Adafruit_PureIO.smbus.SMBus.write_i2c_block_data
documentation: Write a buffer of data to the specified cmd register of the device.

methods.name: Adafruit_PureIO.smbus.SMBus.process_call
documentation:
Perform a smbus process call by writing a word (2 byte) value to
the specified register of the device, and then reading a word of response
data (which is returned).

I2C interface that mimics the Python SMBus API but is implemented with
pure Python calls to ioctl and direct /dev/i2c device access.
pylint: enable=attribute-defined-outside-init
Create an interface that mimics the Python SMBus API.
